calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-12-2017 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Bifurcated Assignment Issue
Off Calendar May-15-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Pamela Fairly appeared by telephone Richard Opper (Petitioner).
Paul Malone for Borg Orla Moeller (Heir).
Tracy Potts appeared by telephone for Brandenburger and Davis.

The parties confirmed that Hoerner Bank gets 10% of the fees and Brandenburger and Davis gets 15%. Ms. Potts explained services performed by Brandenburger and Davis to support fees. Mr. Malone stated that fees are going to split amongst the people who did the work and that his fees are $7,000.00. The Court granted the assignment payment (subject to receipt and review of the order). Counsel to provide a form of order.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Bifurcated Assignment Issue
Off Calendar Mar-08-2017 Continued To Apr-05-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-23-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Pamela Fairley appeared by telephone for Richard Opper (Petitioner).
Paul Malone appeared by telephone for Bent Honore (Claimant).

The Court questioned the percentages reflected in the petition with respect to the assignment, stated that it will distribute everything to the Heirs, that it will hold the 25% to the Bank and questioned the what the fee agreement is. The assignment issue is bifurcated and set for March 8, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Fairley is to prepare a revised order. The Court granted the Petition for Final Distribution (subject to receipt and review of the revised order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-10-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Pamela Fairley (via court call) for (petitioner) Richard Opper.

The Court expressed concern that if it grants the petition a genealogical company will come forward. Ms. Fairley stated that they could do more to find the heirs, that they will conduct a further search, that they will give notice to the Attorney General and that they would like an appointment. The Court stated that the petition for letters of administration will be granted subject to an additional search by the petitioner and not by an heir finder company. Ms. Fairley reported that they discovered additional assets, that they need a bond in a higher amount but did not amend the petition. The Court confirmed that the bond information on the proposed order accurately reflects the assets.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
